C[GreenAsJade: I feel very lost without analysis tool!
C[BennyLava: good! you're here to read!
C[GreenAsJade: Where did you play before OGS?

C[BennyLava: normally on KGS. But I rarely play online. In fact I haven't been playing, except for 4 or 5 games in the last 2 weeks, since November last year. Had lots of work at work, at home and then my second son was born on July 3rd :)
BennyLava: But I have never been playing online very much. I'm rather playing in rl
GreenAsJade: Yay you have a new son - I hope that's all going well.  You are right in the ""hard years""!
BennyLava: but since my first kid I only had the time for playing every so often so I needed to find a solution where I could maybe even play at work. And then came OGS :)
BennyLava: Hehe yeah, I suppose I am. My first son is 4 1/2 years and through the worst. Now its back to the beginning with the new one :P
C[GreenAsJade: Exactly - the really hard years!   But of course good years too, as well as hard.  My kids are 19 and 21 :)
GreenAsJade: I would like to play live or real time, but correspondence like this is just easier to fit in
GreenAsJade: I really like having lots of moves in a row though - thanks for that!
C[BennyLava: 19 and 21, so they'll probably move out soon if they didn't already. Luckily that is still way ahead of me :)

C[GreenAsJade: We are very glad that they are still with us.  They are both at university now (3rd and 1st year).   By this time I was out of home, and thought my kids would be, but we like having them around :)
GreenAsJade: It might be selfish - sometimes I wonder that: should we kick them out for their life experience.
GreenAsJade: But eh!   Time enough for that :)
C[GreenAsJade: Are you actually in Germany?   It seems strange to have a Benny Lava fan in Germany, and your English seems really good ...
BennyLava: nah, I think its prefectly fine still living at home with 19 and 21. I myself was living at home until I was 24 :)
BennyLava: Yep, near Frankfurt. If there weren't any trees I could actually see the airport from here
BennyLava: Thanks! So my ""training"" pays off it seems
GreenAsJade: Do you mean ""English language training""?
GreenAsJade: If so - then yes, I can't tell so far that you aren't a native speaker.  Comes back to my quesiton - are you actually in Germany, German?
C[BennyLava: lol. Yes, I'm a German. Born and raised in Frankfurt
GreenAsJade: Cool!   I have only been in Frankfurt airport, which is great.   I spent a little more time in Munich.

BennyLava: Its just that I like the language. Mostly everything I watch and read is in English and there is Go. I.e., when you're at the European Congress, you are also speaking English most of the time. At least if you are not exclusively hanging with your kin
GreenAsJade: Well, I can't tell that's for sure :)
BennyLava: Frankfurt is ok I guess. But if I had to choose I might even consider moving to Munich
GreenAsJade: I guess you played at a club or something, before kids?
BennyLava: hehe, great to hear that!

BennyLava: yes, we have regular club in the middle of Frankfurt, every Thursday. Usually also on Sundays but the cafe we were meeting at changed their opening hours and are closing at 3pm already. Looking for a new sunday solution atm
C[GreenAsJade: Wow, who closes their cafe and kicks out the Go club!?
BennyLava: hehe, its not that they closed the cafe. Its just that there aren't many people there on Sundays, go players included. I personally would've done the same
C[GreenAsJade: Man you go players needed to buy more coffees :)
BennyLava: lol. That is probably true :) Go players don't really tend to spend lots of money for drinks in cafes and pubs. Even though most of them certainly have the money
C[GreenAsJade: Curse that Kobyashi!     Bed time for me - have to sleep on it!
C[GreenAsJade: How competitive is your Go club?

BennyLava: Its not really about competition. Its more like a regular meeting to be able to play Go in real life. We have a German Go League though. Its kind of a bit like football leagues with 1st all the way through to the 5th league. If you are building a new team and want to compete you always have to start in the 5th league, no matter the strengh of the players. We as the team from Frankfurt managed to rise into the 2nd league as of the next season
BennyLava: There is nothing to win though. If I'm informed correctly I think the winner of the first league is gifted a voucher for Go books but other than that its only for the fun of it :)
GreenAsJade: Sure :)  Knowledge of a job well done
BennyLava: and its really fun to be able to play competitive Go without having to drive or fly somewhere far away.
BennyLava: games are ones per month. Every second or third Thursday, starting at 08:30 pm of our German local time. Most of the games are played on KGS
